description: Open-source, local-first project memory and custom AI workflows for the coding agent you already use. Keeps decisions, gotchas, handoffs, and guardrails portable across Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, and more.
rules: Install or upgrade with the package manager that already owns the existing `prjct` binary, then run `prjct setup` and (in a git repo) `prjct sync`. Built-in update path: `prjct update` (alias `prjct upgrade`) — it auto-detects the package manager (npm/pnpm/bun/yarn/homebrew), pins the exact registry-latest version, consolidates parallel installs, and restarts the daemon. Flags: `--dry-run`, `--yes`/`-y`, `--cleanup`/`--no-cleanup`, `--md`. A non-blocking banner advises `Run: prjct upgrade` when a new version is detected (24h-cached, never blocks).

prjct detects its execution environment automatically with NO user configuration. THE MECHANISM: `core/infrastructure/agent-detector.ts` `isClaudeEnvironment()` returns true for CLAUDE on the FIRST match of, in order (result cached for the process): (1) env var `CLAUDE_AGENT` or `ANTHROPIC_CLAUDE` set by Claude's runtime; (2) `global.mcp` present or `MCP_AVAILABLE` set; (3) a `CLAUDE.md` in the cwd; (4) a `~/.claude/` directory; (5) the cwd path contains `/.claude/` or `/claude-workspace/`. PLAIN-TERMINAL detection is precisely the absence of all five — the Terminal/CLI profile is the default fallback (no signal needed). OUTPUT then adapts with no flag via three host-set signals: `process.stdout.isTTY` (true → animated spinner; false → single static `⚡ prjct …` line) in `core/utils/output.ts`; the LLM-context gate `isLlmContext = !process.stdin.isTTY || options.md || options.json` in `core/index.ts` (commands marked `requiresLlm` refuse a raw human terminal but run transparently inside an agent because piped stdin makes `stdin.isTTY` false); and explicit `--md`/`--json` which strip branding for machine output. Every signal (env var, PATH, TTY, piped stdio) is set by the host, never declared by the user. See docs/environments.md.

OpenAI Codex is detected via the `codex` CLI binary on PATH (a leftover `~/.codex/` directory alone is NOT enough). Codex's project context file is `AGENTS.md` and its skills live in `.agents/skills/` (project) or `~/.codex/skills/` (global). In a Codex sandbox prjct emits the same non-interactive, machine-readable output as any non-TTY agent: static one-line status (no animated spinner), structured text, prompts suppressed; pass `--md` for fully markdown-structured output.

Output adapts in three tiers, no flags required: (1) TTY — animated branded spinner + colors for humans; (2) non-TTY (Claude Code, Codex, CI, pipes) — a single static `⚡ prjct …` line, no animation; (3) `--md` / `--json` — branding stripped, machine-structured output. Commands marked `requiresLlm` are blocked in a raw human terminal unless `--md`/`--json` is passed or they are run inside an agent (stdin not a TTY).

Specifically for Claude Code, once detected (env var / MCP / CLAUDE.md / ~/.claude/) with piped non-TTY stdio, prjct tailors output on every axis with no flag: (a) a single STATIC `⚡ prjct …` status line, never the animated carriage-return spinner, so the transcript stays clean; (b) interactive prompts/confirmations suppressed (nothing blocks on stdin the agent can't answer); (c) `requiresLlm` commands run transparently because piped stdin already makes `isLlmContext` true; (d) with `--md` the branding header/footer is stripped to pure structured markdown the model consumes directly; (e) the installed hooks inject persona + active task + topical memory at SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit and the lookup-first protocol points Claude at the regenerated `~/Documents/prjct/<slug>/_generated/` vault before it re-reads source.

Data lives in three places, not one: (a) in-repo `.prjct/prjct.config.json` — small and COMMITTABLE (projectId + persona + optional `vaultPath`); the `.prjct/` directory itself is gitignored. (b) State (tasks, memory, events, analysis) — global SQLite at `~/.prjct-cli/projects/<projectId>/prjct.db`, per-device, override with `PRJCT_CLI_HOME`. (c) The human/agent-readable vault — `~/Documents/prjct/<slug>/_generated/` (v2.2.0+). SQLite is the source of truth; the vault is a regenerated snapshot — never hand-edit it.

Quickly find a project's local `.prjct/` directory: it is always at the repo root (`<repoRoot>/.prjct/`), created by `prjct init` or the first `prjct` command, and it is .gitignore'd (so `git status` never shows it — that is why it seems hidden). Locate it with `ls -la .prjct/` from the root, or `ls -la "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.prjct/"` from any subdirectory; read it with `cat .prjct/prjct.config.json`; `git check-ignore -v .prjct` shows the rule that hides it; `prjct doctor` confirms it is initialized. In code the path is `pathManager.getLocalConfigPath(projectPath)` = `path.join(projectPath, '.prjct', 'prjct.config.json')` — strictly relative to the project, no env var, no global lookup. The `projectId` inside then maps to the global DB `~/.prjct-cli/projects/<projectId>/prjct.db` and the vault `~/Documents/prjct/<slug>/_generated/` (slug = repo dir name lowercased; `PRJCT_CLI_HOME` relocates the global store). See docs/storage-and-paths.md.

Team / version control: commit `.prjct/prjct.config.json` for a stable shared project identity; never commit state — it is not in the repo (it is per-device SQLite under `~/.prjct-cli`). Multi-developer/multi-device coordination is via optional cloud sync (`prjct login` then `prjct sync`, backend api.prjct.app) using monotonic event IDs; git never carries project state. Sibling git worktrees of one repo share a single vault.

Add a development task with `prjct task "<description>"` run from the repo: it registers the task in SQLite, creates a `task/<slug>` branch, and marks it active (output: `⚡ prjct ✓ Task started: <desc> · branch: task/<slug> · status: active`). Follow with `prjct tag type:feature domain:auth` (categorize), `prjct status done` (close), `prjct ship` (version+commit+PR). `prjct task` with no argument prints the active task. Initialize/register a project first with `prjct sync` (auto-runs on the first prjct command in a git repo) or `prjct init`. Inside an agent you do NOT type commands — say `p. task "..."` (Claude) or `/task "..."` (Cursor/Windsurf).

Get AI assistance for a coding problem by describing it in natural language inside Claude Code (or any wired agent); prjct maps the intent to a quality workflow and runs its methodology, persisting findings to memory: "review my changes" → `review` (Production Bug Hunt + Completeness Gate over the diff); "investigate why tests flake" → `investigate` (Iron Law: root cause before fix); "is this safe to ship?" → `security` (OWASP Top 10 + STRIDE); "qa the checkout page" → `qa` (real browser, regression tests). Also ask "what patterns does this project use?" to make the agent read `~/Documents/prjct/<slug>/_generated/patterns.md` instead of grepping source (lookup-first). Outside an agent, add `--md` to any command for agent-ready markdown.

Triage before ceremony: most work is SIMPLE — go direct (`prjct task` → implement → `qa`/`review` → `ship`), no spec. Reserve `prjct spec` + `audit-spec` for genuinely complex, multi-file, or irreversible work. Over-routing simple work through specs is the main failure mode.
